refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting achievement of a productivity target. It is expressed in terms of what the system must do (support achieving productivity targets) without specifying how. The units (m³/h or ton/h) are part of the measurement context, not implementation details. Therefore, the FR is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection that the NGHE solution enables achieving or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target over the agreed evaluation period under representative conditions.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in mass per hour.

candidate_dps: DP1: Provide a configurable throughput control mechanism to adjust processing rate dynamically.
DP2: Implement a real-time productivity monitoring and feedback system to track and report output against target.
DP3: Design modular processing units that can be scaled or combined to meet varying productivity targets.
DP4: Integrate adaptive scheduling and resource allocation to optimize productivity under varying conditions.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement a real-time productivity monitoring and feedback system to track and report output against target.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between throughput control and monitoring systems could reduce modularity.
coupling_check.notes: Selecting DP2 focuses on monitoring and feedback, which can be designed independently from physical throughput mechanisms, supporting decoupling and modularity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate and timely data for productivity measurement.
Risk that monitoring alone does not guarantee meeting productivity targets without effective control mechanisms.
Assumes operational policies and safety plans do not restrict dynamic adjustments to productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for throughput control or adjustment mechanisms to complement monitoring, ensuring both measurement and control aspects are addressed for achieving productivity targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for managing and integrating productivity monitoring and feedback functionalities across the platform.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from vehicle and other subsystems to monitor productivity metrics and sends processed feedback data.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with task management to align productivity targets and report progress.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with remote monitoring and control centers for real-time updates and feedback.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the productivity monitoring logically fits within the overall solution management; some diffusion due to data exchange with Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Partial containment possible as vehicle generates telemetry data, but implementing monitoring and feedback here would cause diffusion to other modules.
Limited containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than productivity monitoring, leading to high diffusion.
Poor containment; safety services are unrelated to productivity monitoring, causing high diffusion if included.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the real-time productivity monitoring and feedback system, as it centralizes data processing and feedback management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential data latency or bottlenecks if NGHE Solution becomes a single point for all telemetry processing.
Dependency on timely and reliable data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause coupling issues.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with multiple responsibilities if not properly modularized internally.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP2 directly addresses the FR by implementing a real-time productivity monitoring and feedback system that tracks and reports output against the customer-defined productivity target, fulfilling the requirement to support achieving the productivity target measured in volume or mass per hour.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists due to data exchange with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, but the NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the DP.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary, centralizing the productivity monitoring and feedback system. Containment scores indicate high cohesion (8) and low diffusion (2) within this module, minimizing diffusion risks compared to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with telemetry, tasking, and network link to collect and process real-time data, enabling measurement of productivity against targets during representative operations as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces and ownership boundaries defined, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing the NGHE Solution internally to mitigate risks of overloading with multiple responsibilities.
Address potential data latency or bottlenecks by optimizing telemetry data processing or distributing load if necessary.
Manage dependencies on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules carefully to reduce coupling and ensure timely, reliable data exchange.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear traceability, high cohesion, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module is well-defined as the implementation module, and the design parameter directly addresses the functional requirement. Although some recommended revisions exist to improve modularity and manage dependencies, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and manageable design is crucial, and the current level sufficiently captures the necessary detail to proceed with implementation and verification.

revise.why: Consider modularizing the NGHE Solution internally to mitigate risks of overloading with multiple responsibilities and optimize telemetry data processing to address potential data latency or bottlenecks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be internally modularized without altering the current FR->DP mapping.
Dependencies on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be managed effectively through interface design.
open_risks: Potential data latency or bottlenecks in telemetry data processing.
Coupling and dependency risks with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Performance metrics demonstrating real-time data processing meets latency requirements.
Verification results confirming productivity monitoring accuracy against targets during representative operations.
